@conspirator0 Happy Valentine's Day
The only difference is that one of us lived our life online and on Twitter, and one of us didn't. And I guess it's become clear who gets to be successful in our new world.
This is not an excuse to say despicable things. But I know for a fact that every single person on here did something in 2013 that they regret and would not do again today.
I have been on Twitter for 10 years, since I was a freshman in High School! You want me to stand behind every single thought I've had for the past 10 years?

You guys can ignore the history of hacker culture all you want, but you literally live your lives in a world created by our tribe. We are natives here. We have been here longer. There are and always have been different rules and different cultures.

@visakanv None of his "fact checks" actually directly refute the content written, particularly when much of what he takes issue with is statements and quotes from people interviewed by the writer.

@visakanv That's how you get this, Balaji does not appear to be acting in good faith, if he wanted to set the record straight, he could have given comment and helped the reporter understand it – that's how 99% of people handle this, they don't dunk on the reporter on Twitter.

@visakanv Editor: "How is the coronavirus affecting Silicon Valley?
Reporter: "I'll call around and find out"
Reporter: *contacts a bunch of people including public health officials and prominent SV people*
Reporter: *publishes what they say*
@TravelingAnna make a tik tok of it (am I 2020'ing right?)
It's worth saying that part of the reason that this doesn't happen more is modern CMSes suck and don't make this as easy as it should be, which it's own conversation

There's a whole conspiracy theory aesthetic that comes from trying to piece information together from publicly available documents – there's a reason those things exist, they feel a vacuum for people who are reasonably skeptical and ignorant of modern newsgathering practices

All published journalism should have the equivalent of a GitHub repo with the source data/docs/notes available, this is the fundamental difference that most big news orgs don't get, the modern news consumer wants to see everything and wants to see journalists show their work https://t.co/V75a92gFWd

Not feeling well at all. I dunno what it's about.
Ben and I go back to Ben's, watch Alpha Dog. I pass out in the middle of Alpha Dog. Odd, as it is possibly my favorite movie.
We push the car back to John's (slowly, on flat tires) and call new rides home. John looks at us and shakes his head. I skateboard.
As we were driving off from John's (headlights off, stealth mode) we hit a storm drain. BAM BAM. "What was that?" - Get out. 2 tires flat.
We decided it would be a good idea to get saran wrap, and wrap across people's driveways. We did, drove to johns, wrapped around 2 trees.
So last night, we were bored, and Pete got a Bill Clinton mask, and we walked around town looking into bar windows and amusing ourselves...
Home. Last night was... adventerous.
Good day spend with Samara. Bringing her home now. =]
